description:
This image presents two charts related to CORINE Land Cover (CLC) in Europe.
The left chart is a bar chart displaying the "CORINE landcover class distribution in Europe". The X-axis represents "CLC class" from 0 to 40, and the Y-axis shows "% of area" from 0.00% to 25.00%. The highest percentage of area is observed around CLC class 12, representing approximately 21.5% of the total area. Other significant classes include CLC class 23 (approximately 8.0%), CLC class 20 (approximately 7.5%), CLC class 26 (approximately 6.0%), and CLC class 32 (approximately 5.5%). Many CLC classes show a percentage of area below 1%.

The right chart is a scatter plot showing the distribution of "PS/km2" (Persistent Scatterer density per square kilometre) against "CLC class". The X-axis represents "CLC class" from 0 to 40. The Y-axis, labelled "PS/km2", is a logarithmic scale ranging from 100 to 10000. Data points are shown in black, red, and purple. Several groups of CLC classes are highlighted and labelled:
*   "Continuous urban fabric" (CLC classes 0-2) shows the highest PS density, with many points near 10000 PS/km².
*   "Discontinuous urban fabric and other anthropogenic classes" (CLC classes 2-10) also exhibit high PS density, predominantly between 1000 and 10000 PS/km².
*   "Agriculture" (CLC classes 15-25) generally shows lower PS density, mostly between 100 and 1000 PS/km².
*   "Forest and grasslands" (CLC classes 25-30) also have lower PS density, largely below 1000 PS/km².
*   "Bare soils and bedrock" (CLC classes 30-35) show moderate to high PS density, with many points between 1000 and 5000 PS/km².
*   "Wetlands" (CLC classes 35-40) are characterized by moderate PS density, mainly between 100 and 1000 PS/km².
*   "Water bodies" (CLC classes 40-44) typically have the lowest PS density, primarily between 100 and 500 PS/km², with fewer scatter points.

In summary, the urban and bare soil/bedrock CLC classes demonstrate significantly higher Persistent Scatterer densities compared to agricultural, forest, grassland, wetland, or water body classes across Europe.
